title: Jeremiah Poope takes us on the Freedom Trail for America’s 250th (yes, that’s really his name)
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jeremiah Poope, a guide for 23 years, leads the Boston Freedom Trail tour despite high temperatures.
Poope, from Worcester, chose his real name over portraying historical figures to engage young students.
The guide incorporates humor and personal stories into tours, connecting visitors with historical human stories.

title: 60 cars burn in fire at Mass. auto auction site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fire destroyed 50 to 60 cars at Insurance Auto Auctions in Templeton early Thursday morning.
Firefighters responded at 2 a.m. and contained the blaze by 6 a.m.; no injuries were reported.
The fire occurred hours before a scheduled auction, but no auction was held; the incident is under investigation.

title: Boston hit 100. See what meteorologists are saying about chances for triple digits Friday and the July 4 forecast.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston hit 100 degrees Thursday, breaking the daily record high of 98 set in 1963.
Friday's heat indices expected to reach 100-115 degrees, with potential for severe thunderstorms.
Independence Day forecast shows heat indices of 95-100+ degrees and continued overnight heat.

title: Windhorst: Celtics didn’t think Jaylen Brown was their best player last season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics believed Derrick White had a better season than Jaylen Brown in 2025-26, according to Brian Windhorst.
Jaylen Brown set career highs in points, rebounds, and assists, leading Celtics despite Jayson Tatum's absence.
Boston traded Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and multiple draft picks amid analytics-driven scrutiny.

title: ‘I love you JB’: 6-year-old Celtics fan’s tearful message to Jaylen Brown goes viral
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 6-year-old Celtics fan's tearful video message to Jaylen Brown went viral after his trade to the 76ers.
The video shows young Gio, in a signed Celtics jersey, expressing love and gratitude towards Brown.
Jaylen Brown responded to the viral post, reassuring the boy they would remain friends with a heartfelt comment.

title: 4 things to know about Paul George, reportedly coming to the Celtics in the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics acquired Paul George and several draft picks in a trade sending Jaylen Brown to the 76ers.
Paul George, at 36, is a nine-time All-Star with an average of 20.5 points and 6.2 rebounds per game.
Despite frequent playoff appearances, George has never played in the NBA Finals, unlike Jaylen Brown.

title: ‘To the people of Boston, thank you’: Jaylen Brown’s statement after reportedly being traded by the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown released a statement after being reportedly traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown expressed mixed emotions about the trade, highlighting gratitude and respect for the Boston community and fans.
He looks forward to joining Philadelphia, aiming to earn respect through hard work with his new team.

title: Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Connecticut jam band Goose performed at Leader Bank Pavilion amid 100-degree heat, focusing on older hits over new album tracks.
Goose's setlist included 'Iguana Song,' 'Electric Avenue,' and the title track from 'Big Modern!', with vibrant light displays enhancing the show.
The diverse audience featured fans in Grateful Dead, Neil Young, and Celtics apparel, dancing through hours of music despite the heat.

title: Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular will take place on July 4, 2026, at the DCR Hatch Shell, starting at 7 p.m.
Television broadcast of the event will be on Boston's WHDH-TV Channel 7 from 7 p.m. to 9:30 p.m.
Performers include Lainey Wilson, Chance The Rapper, with Jane Lynch hosting and fireworks at 9:15 p.m. accompanied by the Boston Pops.

title: In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Former North Andover officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons discusses being shot by a colleague and her trial acquittal on a new podcast.
Fitzsimmons was on leave for postpartum depression when the shooting occurred; she disputes claims she pointed a gun at Officer Noonan.
She criticizes Judge McCarthy-Neyman as 'evil' for ordering her back to jail despite her injuries affecting breathalyzer compliance.
